title: campanula collina subsp. collina
intro: Campanula collina subsp. collina, also known as the alpine bellflower, is a species of flowering plant in the bellflower family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and to treat kidney stones, due to its high content of flavonoids and glycosides. The health benefits of Campanula collina subsp. collina include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Campanula collina subsp. collina can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
